hypothesis: Proxy-K crossing 10% URGENT threshold correlates with measurable regime shift in maintenance behavior (compaction cadence, DUE spikes).
expect: Regime shift at proxy-K >10%: compaction cadence increases 2x+, DUE items spike. If no regime shift, threshold is arbitrary.

results.urgent_signal_note: 0 URGENT in 30-session maintenance-outcomes window. Dirty-tree condition (56/58 measurements) silences the signal.
results.root_cause: maintenance.py fires URGENT only on clean trees (dirty_tree=False). At N>=2 concurrency, trees are always dirty. 10% threshold is structurally unreachable.
results.due_item_pattern: DUE items present every session (0-13 range) regardless of proxy-K level. No correlation with threshold crossing.

verdict: FALSIFIED — 10% URGENT threshold has zero behavioral effect. Actual compaction regime shift at ~60-70% drift. Root cause: dirty-tree condition silences the signal in concurrent sessions (56/58 dirty).
prescription: Decouple URGENT from dirty-tree condition, or replace fixed 10% with empirical trigger at ~50% drift.
